extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT Wout won the reduced sprint after LAPORTE Christopher single-handedly chased down CAMPENAERTS Victor's late attack with 1km to go.
tactical_patterns: INEOS sent LAPORTE solo to chase dangerous CAMPENAERTS move at 1km, burning domestique instead of sprinter HAYTER's lead-out.
Jumbo-Visma had KWIATKOWSKI do VAN AERT's lead-out from reduced group, exploiting opponents' depleted trains after late chase.
Final categorized climb crested 20km from finish with additional uncategorized climbing after, breaking GROENEWEGEN's preparation.
BikeExchange pushed tempo on final climb for HAYTER, treating versatile classics rider as viable sprint option.

rider_signals.LAPORTE Christopher: positive: Solo chase of CAMPENAERTS at 1km showed exceptional strength, sacrificing HAYTER's lead-out to control race
rider_signals.CAMPENAERTS Victor: positive: Late solo attack with 1km remaining was dangerous enough to force INEOS into emergency response
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: neutral: Had KWIATKOWSKI doing lead-out but couldn't match VAN AERT's power despite team controlling final climb
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: Team pulled plug on sprint effort over 2 minutes down, terrain with uncategorized climbing post-KOM unsuitable
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michal: positive: Led out VAN AERT despite riding for different team, suggesting loan or tactical cooperation arrangement
